description: SemVer 2.0.0 precedence and §7.1 tag-grammar vectors for SemVer-Trust (spec/semver-trust.md §7.1, §7.2). 'precedence' vectors are strictly ascending ordered lists (a harness parses each entry, sorts, and compares to the given order). 'grammar' vectors map a tag string to a parse outcome and, for parseable tags, its components.

vectors.description: The canonical SemVer 2.0.0 §11 precedence example; anchors the comparator against the authoritative source.
vectors.note: Numeric identifiers compare numerically (beta.2 < beta.11); a smaller set of pre-release fields ranks lower when prefixes are equal (alpha < alpha.1); pre-release ranks below the release (rc.1 < 1.0.0).

vectors.description: A trust-gated release outranks an rc among pre-releases, and both rank below the clean release.
vectors.note: "rc" < "t1" because ASCII 'r' (114) < 't' (116); the clean 1.4.0 outranks every pre-release.

vectors.description: Iterations are SemVer numeric identifiers and compare numerically, not lexically.
vectors.note: 2 < 10 numerically; a lexical comparison would wrongly order 10 < 2.

vectors.description: Across trust levels at the same core, the trust identifier decides before the iteration is consulted.
vectors.note: First identifier "t0" < "t2" (ASCII '0' < '2'); the iterations .5 and .1 are never compared.

vectors.description: Raw-SemVer hazard: lexical comparison of alphanumeric identifiers orders "t10" below "t2".
vectors.note: This is a true SemVer ordering and it exercises the implementation's comparator, NOT grammar acceptance: the §7.1 grammar caps levels at a single digit precisely to foreclose this hazard, so 't10' is never a valid trust identifier (see grammar/invalid/two-digit-level).

vectors.description: SemVer §11: a numeric pre-release identifier has lower precedence than an alphanumeric one.
vectors.note: "1" is numeric, "rc" is alphanumeric; numeric always sorts below alphanumeric.

vectors.description: Core MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH compares numerically, field by field.
vectors.note: PATCH, then MINOR, then MAJOR differences each raise precedence.

vectors.description: The full trust ladder at one core version; every trust-gated release sorts below the clean release.
vectors.note: "t0" < "t1" < "t2" < "t3" lexically, and all rank below the clean release: low-trust releases are opt-in by construction (§7.1).

vectors.description: Core precedence dominates the trust suffix.
vectors.note: A T0 pre-release of a higher core (1.0.1) outranks a T3 pre-release of a lower core (1.0.0); the trust level never reorders across core versions.

vectors.description: A trust-version with no component path.
A trust-version with a single-segment component path.
A clean (no trust suffix) trust-version with a nested component path.
A clean trust-version with no component path (clean channel).
Level 0 is a valid trust level.
Level 3 is a valid trust level.
Iterations are SemVer numeric identifiers and may have multiple digits.
A two-digit level is not a valid trust identifier.

expected.reason: level must be a single digit 0-3; "t10" has two digits (§7.1)

vectors.description: A trust identifier without an iteration is incomplete.
Iterations start at 1; zero is not allowed.

expected.reason: iteration starts at 1; "0" is not allowed (§7.1)

vectors.description: Only levels 0-3 are defined.

expected.reason: level out of range; only 0-3 are defined (§7.1)

vectors.description: The core-version must be MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH; a two-field version is not a valid tag.

expected.reason: core-version must be MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH; "1.4" is not (§7.1)

vectors.description: A valid SemVer version whose pre-release is not trust-shaped parses as a plain pre-release version with the trust suffix absent (not a trust-version, not rejected).
